title: Automatic identification and analysis of a tissue sample
abstract: Methods and apparatus for automatically analyzing a region of interest within a tissue sample are provided. The method includes obtaining an input image of the tissue sample, the input image comprising a plurality of pixels; applying a classifier to the input image to identify a plurality of candidate pixels based on the plurality of pixels of the input image; applying at least one rule to the plurality of candidate pixels to determine a region of interest; and determining at least one feature within the region of interest.
claims:
Claims What is claimed is: CLAIMS 1. A method of automatically analyzing a region of interest within a tissue sample, the method comprising: obtaining an input image of the tissue sample, the input image comprising a plurality of pixels; applying a classifier to the input image to identify a plurality of candidate pixels based on the plurality of pixels of the input image; applying at least one rule to the plurality of candidate pixels to determine a region of interest; and determining at least one feature within the region of interest. 2. The method of claim 1, wherein applying the classifier comprises applying the classifier to each pixel of a plurality of pixels one at a time. 3. The method of claim 1, wherein the classifier is a random forest classifier. 4. The method of claim 1, wherein the classifier is a trained classifier trained using a plurality of training images known to contain at least one target cell type. 5. The method of claim 4, wherein the classifier uses a plurality of parameters. 6. The method of claim 5, wherein the plurality of parameters include parameters selected from the group consisting of (i) a parameter based on pixel color, (ii) a parameter based on pixel intensity, (iii) a parameter based on whether a pixel belongs to an edge, (iv) a parameter based on whether a pixel is indicative a particular texture, (v) a parameter based on the characteristics of an individual pixel, and (vi) a parameter based on the characteristics of a plurality of neighboring pixels. 7. The method of claim 5, wherein the plurality of parameters are based on a cell type being analyzed. 8. The method of claim 5, wherein the plurality of parameters are based on a tissue type being analyzed. 9. The method of claim 1, wherein obtaining the input image comprises: receiving a preliminary image comprising a plurality of preliminary image pixels, wherein a number of the plurality of preliminary image pixels is greater than a number of the plurality of pixels of the input image; and generating the input image from the preliminary image by segmenting the preliminary image. 10. The method of claim 9, wherein the input image is at least a portion of an image from a microscope. 11. The method of claim 10, further comprising: obtaining the tissue sample; and capturing the input image with the microscope. 12. The method of claim 11, further comprising staining the tissue sample prior to capturing the input image. 13. The method of claim 1, wherein the at least one rule comprises a rule selected from the group consisting of (i) a blurring rule, (ii) a size constraint rule, and (iii) a shape constraint rule. 14. The method of claim 1, further comprising forming a binary image with the plurality of candidate pixels having a first value and pixels of the plurality of pixels that are not classified as the plurality of candidate pixels having a second value. 15. The method of claim 14, wherein applying the at least one rule comprises applying the at least one rule to the binary image. 16. The method of claim 15, wherein applying the at last one rule comprises blurring the binary image. 17. The method of claim 16, wherein blurring the binary image comprises applying a Gaussian blur filter to the binary image. 18. The method of claim 15, wherein applying the at last one rule comprises applying a size constraint to the binary image. 19. The method of claim 18, wherein applying the size constraint to the binary image comprises excluding a contiguous group of pixels having the first value when a number of pixels in the contiguous group of pixels is below a threshold number of pixels. 20. The method of claim 15, wherein applying the at last one rule comprises applying a shape constraint on the binary image. 21. The method of claim 18, wherein applying the shape constraint to the binary image comprises excluding a contiguous group of pixels having the first value when a similarity measure of a shape of the contiguous group of pixels compared to a reference shape is less than a threshold similarity. 22. The method of claim 21, wherein the reference shape is a circle. 23. The method of claim 15, wherein applying the at last one rule comprises forming, from the binary image, a single contiguous group of pixels having the first value. 24. The method of claim 1, wherein the tissue sample is a kidney tissue sample. 25. The method of claim 1, wherein the region of interest is a region of the input image identified as being a cell of a predetermined cell type. 26. The method of 25, wherein the cell type is a glomerulus and the tissue sample is a kidney tissue sample. 27. The method of claim 1, wherein determining at least one feature within the region of interest comprises processing a portion of the input image corresponding to the region of interest. 28. The method of claim 27, wherein the at least one feature comprises mesangial matrix expansion. 29. The method of claim 28, wherein determining the at least one feature comprises determining which pixels of the plurality of pixels within the region of interest have a saturation that exceeds a threshold saturation value. 30. The method of claim 27, wherein the at least one feature comprises a number of nuclei. 31. The method of claim 30, wherein determining the at least one feature comprises determining which pixels of the plurality of pixels within the region of interest have a hue within a predetermined range. 32. The method of claim 27, wherein the at least one feature comprises capillary openness. 33. The method of claim 32, wherein determining the at least one feature comprises determining which pixels of the plurality of pixels within the region of interest have a brightness within a predetermined range. 34. A computing device for automatically analyzing a region of interest within a tissue sample, the computing device comprising: a memory configured to store a classifier and an input image of the tissue sample; and a processor communicatively coupled with the memory and configured to: receive the input image; apply the classifier to the input image to identify at least one candidate region of the input image; apply at least one rule to the at least one candidate region to determine a region of interest; and 

 determine at least one feature within the region of interest. 35. A method of training a classifier for automatically determining a region of interest within a tissue sample, the method comprising: obtaining a plurality of training images, each of the plurality of training images comprising a plurality of pixels and one or more predetermined regions of interest; and training a machine learning classifier using the plurality of training images, wherein the classifier uses a plurality of parameters. 101.
